qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: The octal representation of an integer is . If this were to be treated as an eight-bit integer in an based computer, its decimal equivalent is A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: Suppose the domain set of an attribute consists of signed four digit numbers. What is the percentage of reduction in storage space of this attribute if it is stored as an integer rather than in character form? A. B. C. D.

qids.prose: Consider the values of and the sequence X:= A + B Y:= A + C X:= X + C Y:= Y + B executed on a computer where floating point numbers are represented with bits. The values for and will be A. B. C. D.
